title: Region inference handles longest-pattern without annotation — defend this
description: Ferrum claims 'fn longest(a: &str, b: &str): &str' compiles without lifetime annotation. This is the canonical case Rust requires explicit annotation for. If true, this is the biggest concrete DX win over Rust for borrow-checker code. A reviewer may question whether this is implementable or may suggest adding the annotation for clarity. Defend: the spec's inference algorithm (fresh region per parameter → constraint generation → smallest valid solution) is designed to handle exactly this case. The annotation would be redundant and the compiler warns to remove it. This is correct behavior and should be preserved.
